title: APC InRow and Airflow Unit Compressor or Fan Fault Alarms
exclusions: Out of scope for non-APC chilled-water CRAC strings without traps or polls in the APC enterprise MIB tree, environments where SNMP from NMC to Splunk is disabled and no alternate poller exists, and pure predictive thermal analytics that do not ingest these SNMP feeds. Does not certify compliance with any specific regulation.
evidence: Scheduled saved search or alert "UC_15_1_31_APICoolingFault" against `index=power` with CSV export disabled but email/pdf digest to facilities; paired Dashboard Studio timeline and table panels filtered to `sourcetype=snmp:trap` or `snmp:apc:cooling`, retained per your internal evidence policy.

controlTest.positiveScenario: An APC InRow Network Management Card emits an SNMP trap to the Splunk Connect for SNMP listener carrying enterprise identifiers under `.1.3.6.1.4.1.318` with alarm text referencing a compressor stall or fan fault; within one minute the event indexes on `index=power` as `sourcetype=snmp:trap`, matches `trapoid="*318*"`, and the UC correlation surfaces that host for alerting.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A generic `linkDown` SNMP trap from non-APC infrastructure lands on `index=power` without any `.1.3.6.1.4.1.318` enterprise OID or compressor, fan, InRow, or airflow varbind text; after the UC's `trapoid="*318*"` gate and cooling keyword filters, the search returns zero rows eligible for InRow compressor or fan fault alerting.

dataSources: `index=power` receives APC InRow and NetShelter airflow events as `sourcetype=snmp:trap` (enterprise `.1.3.6.1.4.1.318`) from Splunk Connect for SNMP (Splunkbase #6266) or as polled `sourcetype=snmp:apc:cooling` metrics from the SNMP Modular Input (Splunkbase #1537); key extracted fields include `trapoid`, `trap_name`, `eventName`, and cooling varbinds such as `coolingUnitState` when props are aligned to your MIB.
app: Splunk Connect for SNMP (Splunkbase #6266) trap receiver, SNMP Modular Input (Splunkbase #1537)
spl:
index=power (sourcetype=snmp:trap OR sourcetype=snmp:apc:cooling) host=* trapoid="*318*"
| search cooling OR compressor OR Compressor OR fan OR Fan OR InRow OR Airflow OR airflow OR environmental OR Environmental
| table _time, host, trapoid, trap_name, eventName, _raw
description: APC InRow and aisle containment products emit SNMP traps when compressors stall, fans fail, or airflow is inadequate. Landing those traps in Splunk gives the same early warning as UPS events for thermal runaway in edge and small data halls.
value: Prevents thermal shutdowns of IT loads by correlating cooling faults with rack inlet temperatures before CRAC capacity is exhausted.
implementation: Stand up the trap path first: configure the Splunk Connect for SNMP (6266) receiver to accept UDP/162 from APC Network Management Card sources and confirm events write to `index=power` as `sourcetype=snmp:trap` with parsed `trapoid` and varbind alarm text into a consistent `alarm_text` or CIM-friendly field. For sites that poll instead of trap, define a modular input stanza in the SNMP Modular Input (1537) that walks the APC enterprise cooling OIDs and routes to `sourcetype=snmp:apc:cooling`, extracting `coolingUnitState` and fan or compressor alarm OIDs documented in your MIB reference. Put a maintenance tag or host lookup in front of alerting so vendor-executed compressor tests during change windows do not page the default queue. Bind the alert action to the data-center facilities roster, attach the thermal escalation SOP, and keep a `-15m` dispatch window aligned to the cron schedule to match the SPL in this UC.
visualization: Dashboard Studio timeline of cooling trap volume on index=power by host; primary table sorted by _time with columns host, trapoid, trap_name, eventName, and parsed alarm_text; single-value KPI for distinct faulting InRow hosts in the last 15 minutes; markdown panel with NMC verification checklist for drilldown; optional Sankey or relationship visualization from trap host to row or aisle using an asset lookup.
knownFalsePositives: (1) APC Network Management Card during authorized firmware reload emits multiple cold-start and environmental test traps‚Äîsuppress when `host` matches the `facilities_maintenance` lookup active window for that PDU/NMC pair. (2) Splunk Connect for SNMP duplicate deliveries from redundant trap forwarders double-count the same `_raw`‚Äîdeduplicate using `streamstats` on a hash of host+trapoid+varbind payload within a 120s pane. (3) Row-based InRow units under load bank or disaster-recovery rehearsal show brief compressor surge alarms that match keywords but are benign‚Äîexclude when a `change_ticket` field or manual `dr_drill=true` marker is present on related change data. (4) Stale `snmp:apc:cooling` poll results after a VLAN outage look like sustained faults until the next successful walk‚Äîconfirm with `_indextime` lag or a secondary `ping` lookup before paging after partitions.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Splunk Enterprise 9.2+ or Splunk Cloud search tier with a Heavy Forwarder or dedicated data-collection node hosting either Splunk Connect for SNMP (Splunkbase #6266) or the SNMP Modular Input (Splunkbase #1537).
‚Ä¢ RBAC: use a `splunk_admin` (or deployment-manager equivalent) principal to install apps and open `inputs.conf`; grant the operations team a custom role with read access to `index=power` and `edit_monitor` disabled in production.
‚Ä¢ Network/firewall: allow SNMP traps (UDP/162) from APC InRow/NMC subnets to the collector; for polling, allow SNMP GET/GET-NEXT (UDP/161) outbound from Splunk to each NMC.

Configure data collection
‚Ä¢ Trap path: bind the SC4SNMP listener to the correct interface, register each APC device IP, and verify the first replayed trap shows `sourcetype=snmp:trap` on `index=power` with `trapoid` containing `.1.3.6.1.4.1.318`.
‚Ä¢ Enable rich parsing (`full_json`, `VARBINDS`, or equivalent) so `trap_name`, `eventName`, and raw varbind strings are searchable for keywords like compressor, fan, airflow, or InRow.
‚Ä¢ Polling path: create an SNMP Modular Input profile with a 60‚Äì120s interval, normalize output to `sourcetype=snmp:apc:cooling`, and confirm `coolingUnitState` plus fault OIDs populate after a walk of the APC enterprise branch.

Create and tune the correlation
‚Ä¢ Start from the packaged SPL baseline: constrain to `host=*`, `trapoid="*318*"`, and (for dual path) `(sourcetype=snmp:trap OR sourcetype=snmp:apc:cooling)`.
‚Ä¢ Add `streamstats` or `transaction` keyed by host and trap signature inside a `-15m` window to collapse flap duplicates before alerting.
‚Ä¢ Optional: `lookup` enrich host -> row ID so dashboards separate aisle containment zones.

Validate
‚Ä¢ Replay a sanitized trap `_raw` sample in search using `index=power sourcetype=snmp:trap` over `-24h@h` and compare timestamps with the APC web UI alarm history on the same NMC.
‚Ä¢ For polled feeds, chart `_time` vs `coolingUnitState` and confirm gaps align with SNMP timeouts rather than silent drops.

Operationalize and troubleshoot
‚Ä¢ Route alerts to facilities on-call with throttle keyed on host + trap signature; attach links to rack inlet temperature dashboards.
‚Ä¢ If dual-published to a BMS, document precedence (Splunk vs BMS source of truth) to avoid duplicate tickets.
‚Ä¢ Common failures: SNMP ACL changes on NMC (symptom: sudden zero events), clock skew (symptom: traps appear outside drill windows), SC4SNMP restart clearing dedup state (symptom: alert storm) ‚Äî capture in the runbook with restart and ACL checks.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the physical data center for power, cooling, environment, and physical access.
